name: Associated Types & Projections
scope_summary: Add associated type declarations to traits, projection types (T::Item), projection equality in unification and type checker. Implement occurs check for projections to prevent cycles. Provide diagnostics for unresolved projections.

tests.V01-T01: Define trait `Iterator { type Item; }` with impl `type Item = u32` → projection `I::Item` resolves to `u32` (run-pass)
tests.V01-T02: Nested projection: `T::Item::Target` where `T::Item` is another trait with associated type → resolves correctly (run-pass)
tests.V01-T03: Unify `I::Item` with `u32` where `I` bound to impl → Ok (run-pass)
tests.V01-T04: Projection in where clause: `fn foo<T>() where T::Item: Clone` → compiles (run-pass)
tests.V01-T06: Cycle detection: `type A = Self::B; type B = Self::A;` → error (compile-fail)
tests.V01-T08: Projection with mismatched trait bound → error (compile-fail)

mocking: No mocking. Extend existing TyCtx and InferenceTable. Use `glyim_test::assert_ty` and `assert_diag_contains`.

name: Where Clauses & Supertraits
scope_summary: Implement parsing, HIR lowering, and type checking of `where` clauses on functions, traits, and impls. Enforce supertrait inheritance (e.g., `trait Copy: Clone` requires `Clone` impl). Propagate implied bounds from supertraits and where clauses.

tests.V02-T01: Function with `where T: Clone` → compiles if T implements Clone (run-pass)
tests.V02-T02: Trait with supertrait, impl must satisfy both → compiles (run-pass)
tests.V02-T03: Where clause on trait impl → constraints enforced (run-pass)

mocking: Extend existing solver to handle inheritance. Use `glyim_test::mock::MockSolver` for testing where clauses in isolation.

name: Default Methods
scope_summary: Allow trait methods to have default implementations. Implement inheritance: impls can override default methods or inherit them. Ensure correct dispatch and that default methods can call other trait methods (including default ones).

tests.V03-T01: Trait with default method, impl without overriding → default used (run-pass)
tests.V03-T03: Default method calling another default method (run-pass)
tests.V03-T05: Default method calls missing method → error (compile-fail)

mocking: Use existing HIR and THIR. No mocking.

name: Coherence & Orphan Rules
scope_summary: Implement orphan rules: impl of a foreign trait on a foreign type is only allowed if at least one local type appears. Check overlap between impls (trait + self type + where clauses). Report conflicts. Support negative impls (e.g., `impl !Send for T`).

tests.V04-T01: Duplicate impl for same type → error (compile-fail)
tests.V04-T02: Impl for foreign trait on foreign type → error unless local type (compile-fail)
tests.V04-T03: Blanket impl conflicts with concrete impl → error (compile-fail)
tests.V04-T04: Valid orphan: impl ForeignTrait for LocalType → compiles (run-pass)
tests.V04-T05: Negative impl `impl !Send for T` → overrides auto trait (run-pass)

mocking: Use TraitContext to register impls and check. Use `glyim_test::mock::TestDbBuilder` to create crates with different local/foreign types.

name: HRTB (Higher‑Ranked Trait Bounds)
scope_summary: Parse and solve `for<'a> Fn(&'a i32)`. Introduce binder handling in unification and trait matching. Support placeholder regions and universes. Integrate with trait solver to prove higher‑ranked bounds.

tests.V05-T01: Function bound with `for<'a> Fn(&'a u32)` → compiles (run-pass)

mocking: Extend unification to handle bound regions. Use `glyim_test::property::check_ty_property` for property‑based testing of binder substitution.

name: Auto Traits (Send, Sync)
scope_summary: Automatically implement marker traits (Send, Sync, Unpin) for types when all field types implement the trait. Support negative impls (`impl !Send for T`) to opt out. Compute auto traits during type collection and store in `TyCtx`. Ensure coherence with manual impls.

tests.V06-T01: Struct with all Send fields → auto impl Send (run-pass)
tests.V06-T02: Struct with non‑Send field → no auto Send (compile-fail if used as Send)
tests.V06-T03: Negative impl `impl !Send for T` → overrides auto (run-pass)
tests.V06-T05: Manual impl of auto trait overrides auto (run-pass)

mocking: Compute during type collection. Use `glyim_test::assert_ty` to check that `TyCtx::ty_flags` includes auto trait markers.

name: Trait Objects
scope_summary: Implement `dyn Trait` types: vtable layout (pointers to methods, drop, size), dynamic dispatch via vtable pointers, code generation for trait object method calls, and upcasting to supertraits. Enforce object safety (no Self: Sized, no generic methods).

tests.V07-T01: Create trait object from concrete type (run-pass)
tests.V07-T04: Object safety check: non‑object‑safe trait → error (compile-fail)
tests.V07-T05: Vtable layout: size and alignment match pointer width (run-pass)

mocking: Use real layout and codegen. Use `glyim_test::assert_layout` to verify vtable layout.

name: Polonius Region Inference (Base)
scope_summary: Implement Polonius‑style region inference: regions as sets of loans, liveness + outlives constraints, Datalog‑like solver using `datafrog`. Replace single‑block analysis with full CFG traversal. Generate constraints from MIR and solve.

tests.V08-T01: Borrow that crosses a basic block (if condition) → no error (run-pass)
tests.V08-T02: Mutable borrow of field used after loop → allowed (run-pass)
tests.V08-T03: Borrow expires after last use in different block → no error (run-pass)

mocking: Integrate with MIR; use `datafrog` for Datalog. Use `glyim_test::snapshot_mir` to verify constraint generation.

name: Two‑Phase Borrows
scope_summary: Support two‑phase borrows: reservation phase acts as shared, activation at call site. Implement `BorrowKind::Mut` with `allow_two_phase_borrow` flag and checking logic (reservation conflicts, activation point).

tests.V09-T02: Two‑phase borrow with intervening shared borrow (run-pass)
tests.V09-T04: Reservation conflict with mutable borrow → error (compile-fail)

mocking: Extend borrowck with activation points. Use `glyim_test::assert_borrow_error`.

name: Move Analysis & Partial Moves
scope_summary: Dataflow analysis to track moved‑out paths; prevent use after move; allow partial moves of struct fields (move one field, keep others usable). Implement path‑sensitive move analysis.

tests.V10-T01: Move of whole struct, field access → error (compile-fail)
tests.V10-T02: Partial move of one field, other fields still usable (run-pass)
tests.V10-T03: Move of copy type is not a move (run-pass)
tests.V10-T04: Move out of array element → error (compile-fail)
tests.V10-T05: Move of field and later reassign → allowed (run-pass)

mocking: Add move dataflow analysis. Use `glyim_test::assert_borrow_error`.

name: Drop Checking & Interior Mutability
scope_summary: Ensure drop order safety (values cannot be used after drop). Recognise `Cell`/`RefCell` as interior mutability and allow shared mutable borrows. Integrate with borrowck and add drop‑flag tracking.

tests.V11-T02: Interior mutability: `Cell<i32>` allows mutation through shared ref (run-pass)
tests.V11-T03: Drop order for structs with mixed fields (run-pass)
tests.V11-T04: Drop flags for conditionally dropped values (run-pass)

mocking: Requires `UnsafeCell` detection. Use `glyim_test::assert_layout` to verify `Cell` layout.

name: LLVM Module & Basic Block Lowering
scope_summary: Create LLVM module, set target triple and data layout. Translate MIR basic blocks and terminators (Goto, Return, Unreachable, SwitchInt) to LLVM IR. Use placeholders for rvalues (to be filled later).

tests.V12-T01: Empty function → LLVM function with `ret void` (run-pass via `lli`)

mocking: Use real MIR from U02. Write tests that compile to LLVM IR and run via `lli` (LLVM interpreter) to verify behaviour.

name: LLVM Rvalue Lowering (Aggregates & Memory Ops)
scope_summary: Lower aggregate rvalues: `Aggregate` (struct/tuple/array literals), `Ref` (address‑of), `Discriminant` (enum tag read), `Len` (array length). Produce LLVM `alloca`, `getelementptr`, `load`/`store`, and constant aggregates.

tests.V13-T04: Discriminant of enum → read tag field (run-pass)
tests.V13-T05: Len of array → constant or `getelementptr` diff (run-pass)

mocking: Uses MIR from U02, layout from V01. Test with `lli`.

name: LLVM Function Calls & ABI
scope_summary: Implement `Call` terminator: direct/indirect calls, argument passing according to target ABI (using `LayoutComputer`), return value handling (including sret for large structs), and unwind/cleanup (basic landingpad).

tests.V14-T03: Pass struct arguments by value according to ABI (run-pass)
tests.V14-T04: Return struct via sret when ABI requires (run-pass)

mocking: Requires `FnAbi` from layout. Use `glyim_test::assert_layout` to verify ABI decisions.

name: LLVM Drop & Deallocation
scope_summary: Generate calls to `core::ptr::drop_in_place` for values going out of scope (Drop terminators). Allocate/deallocate memory using `glyim_alloc`/`glyim_dealloc` (runtime). Implement drop flags and cleanup paths.

tests.V15-T01: Drop of local variable at block end (run-pass)
tests.V15-T02: Drop of boxed value → deallocation call (run-pass)

mocking: Uses runtime alloc/dealloc. Test with `valgrind` or address sanitizer to detect leaks.

name: Core Crate (no_std)
scope_summary: Implement complete `core` library: `option`, `result`, `iter`, `slice`, `str`, `cell`, `mem`, `ptr`, `ops`, `cmp`, `marker`, `panic`, `hint`, `convert`, `default`. Include all lang items and panic handler. Must be compilable with Glyim and pass all tests.

tests.V16-T03: `panic!` macro prints message and aborts (run-pass with expected abort)

mocking: Must compile with Glyim; no mocks. Use `glyim_test::TestRunner` with `--test` flag.

name: Alloc Crate
scope_summary: Implement `alloc` crate: `Vec`, `String`, `Box`, `Rc`, `Arc`, `RawVec`, `alloc::alloc` (global allocator), allocator API (`GlobalAlloc`), `Layout`. Include all methods, tests, and documentation.

tests.V17-T05: Allocator layout and OOM handling (run-pass with expected abort)

mocking: Depends on core and global allocator (glyim-runtime). Use `glyim_test::TestRunner`.

name: Std Crate (I/O, Files, Threads)
scope_summary: Build `std` on top of core/alloc: I/O (`std::io`, `std::fs`, `std::net` basics), threading (`std::thread`), environment (`std::env`), time (`std::time`), process (`std::process`). Uses OS syscalls via FFI (extern "C" stubs).
mocking: Uses OS syscalls via `extern "C"`. For tests, use actual OS calls (no mocks).

name: Declarative Macros (macro_rules!)
scope_summary: Implement full `macro_rules!` with hygiene: token matching (patterns with `$`), repetition (`$(...)*`), expansion, and macro hygiene (marks, transparency). Support `$crate` and macro export/import.
mocking: Use real token streams. Use `glyim_test::snapshot_cst` to test macro expansion results.

name: Build Tool (Glyip)
scope_summary: Create Cargo‑like build system: `glyip new`, `glyip build`, `glyip test`, `glyip run`. Support dependency resolution (Cargo.toml), incremental compilation, and crate caching. Integrate with the compiler.

tests.V20-T01: `glyip new` creates a new crate with correct structure (integration test)
tests.V20-T02: `glyip build` compiles a crate with dependencies (integration test)

mocking: Uses existing compiler via CLI. Write integration tests in `tests/` directory of the `glyip` crate.

name: Language Server Enhancements
scope_summary: Implement full LSP capabilities: go‑to‑definition, hover, completions (with semantic tokens), document symbols, diagnostics on change, and workspace symbol search. Use the compiler's incremental compilation database.

tests.V21-T01: Goto definition for local variable (LSP test)

mocking: Use Database to query analysis. Use `lsp-types` to simulate LSP requests.

name: Mono Item Root Discovery
scope_summary: Implement mono item root discovery: scan crate for entry points (`main`, `#[start]`, `#[no_mangle]` (exported) functions, and reachable statics). Build initial queue of `MonoItem`s (functions, constants, statics).

tests.V22-T01: Crate with `main` function → root item detected (run-pass)
tests.V22-T04: No entry points → no mono items (warning) (compile-pass)

mocking: Uses real HIR and MIR. Use `glyim_test::snapshot_mir` to verify mono item list.

name: Mono Item Graph Traversal & Collection
scope_summary: Implement mono item graph traversal: recursively follow function calls, drop glue, and constant references from collected items. Use worklist algorithm, avoid infinite recursion, and collect all required instantiations.

tests.V23-T01: Call graph: `main` calls `foo<T>` with concrete T → `foo<T>` added (run-pass)

mocking: Uses MIR bodies and substitution. Use `glyim_test::snapshot_mir` to verify final item set.

name: Mono Item Cache & Deduplication
scope_summary: Implement cache that maps `(DefId, Substitution)` to `MonoItemId`. Ensure each instantiation is processed exactly once across the crate. Use hash consing for substitutions.

tests.V24-T01: Same generic instantiation called twice → only one mono item (run-pass)
tests.V24-T03: Cache survives across multiple worklist iterations (run-pass)
tests.V24-T04: Zero memory leaks (no duplicate `Arc<Body>`) (run under valgrind)

mocking: Work with real `DefId`. Use `glyim_test::assert_mir` to compare mono item counts.

name: Codegen Unit Partitioning
scope_summary: Partition mono items into codegen units (CGUs) for parallel code generation. Strategy: function‑level partitioning, collect items by source module, optionally by call graph to minimise cross‑CGU references.

tests.V25-T03: Cross‑module call edge does not force CGU merge (run-pass)

mocking: Uses collected mono items. Use `glyim_test::snapshot` to inspect CGU partitions.

name: Post‑Monomorphization Checks
scope_summary: Run lint‑like checks after monomorphization: detect unused generic parameters (polymorphisation light), detect mono item size blow‑up, and emit errors for unsized locals in instantiated functions.

tests.V26-T01: Generic parameter unused → warning (compile-pass with warning)

mocking: Requires MIR after instantiation. Use `glyim_test::assert_diag_contains`.

name: LLVM ABI‑Aware Function Call Lowering
scope_summary: Use `glyim_layout::FnAbi` to lower function calls and arguments correctly. Implement by‑value passing, indirect (sret) returns, and handling of homogenous aggregates (HVA/HFA) for ARM/x86.

tests.V27-T01: Function returning large struct → sret parameter (run-pass)
tests.V27-T04: ARM HVA: struct of four floats passed in SIMD regs (run-pass)

mocking: Uses `FnAbi` from layout crate. Test with `lli` and verify argument registers.

name: LLVM Rvalue Lowering (Operators & Casts)
scope_summary: Lower remaining rvalues: `BinaryOp` (all integer, float, boolean, bitwise ops), `UnaryOp` (not, neg), `Cast` (int↔int, int↔float, pointer casts). Use LLVM instructions (add, sub, mul, udiv, sdiv, fadd, etc.) and handle overflow checks.

tests.V28-T05: Cast int to float and vice versa (run-pass)
tests.V28-T06: Cast pointer to integer and back (run-pass)
tests.V28-T07: Overflow checks for `+` in debug mode → panic (run-pass with abort)

mocking: Uses real types and values. Test with `lli`.

name: LLVM SwitchInt & Switch Target Lowering
scope_summary: Lower `SwitchInt` terminators to LLVM `switch` instruction, including large number of branches and fallback (otherwise). Optimise small switches (2‑3 cases) to `if` ladder when beneficial.

tests.V29-T01: Switch over small integer range → LLVM switch (run-pass)
tests.V29-T02: Switch with missing values → default branch (run-pass)

mocking: Uses MIR switch constructs. Test with `lli` and verify branch behaviour.

name: LLVM Debug Info (DWARF) Generation
scope_summary: Generate DWARF debug info: compile unit, subprograms, lexical blocks, variables (locations), line numbers. Use LLVM DI builder. Support source file mappings and `-g` flag.

tests.V30-T01: Compile with `-g` → debug info in object file (run‑pass, check with `llvm-dwarfdump`)
tests.V30-T02: Breakpoint on function entry works (run under `lldb` or `gdb`)
tests.V30-T03: Variable location shown in debugger (run under debugger)
tests.V30-T04: Line number stepping matches source (run under debugger)

mocking: LLVM DI builder. Use `llvm-dwarfdump` to verify metadata.

name: Polymorphization (Optional Advanced)
scope_summary: Implement polymorphization: detect generic parameters not used in the function body and avoid monomorphizing over them, reducing code size. Follow rustc's defunct `-Zpolymorphize` design. Optional – can be skipped for v0.2.0.

tests.V31-T01: Function `fn foo<T>(x: i32)` where T unused → not monomorphized over T (run-pass)
tests.V31-T02: Function `fn bar<T>(x: T)` where T used → monomorphized (run-pass)
tests.V31-T03: Polymorphization across multiple calls keeps consistency (run-pass)
tests.V31-T04: Correctness with drop glue (may still need T for drop) (run-pass)

mocking: Uses mono item graph. Use `glyim_test::snapshot` to compare mono item counts with and without flag.

name: LLVM Optimisation Pipeline Integration
scope_summary: Integrate LLVM’s optimisation pipeline: set optimisation level (`-O0`/`-O1`/`-O2`/`-O3`), run corresponding LLVM passes (inlining, mem2reg, loop optimisations, etc.). Use `LLVMPassManagerBuilder` or Inkwell’s `OptimizationLevel`.

tests.V32-T02: `-O2` → inlining and mem2reg performed (run-pass, check generated assembly)

mocking: LLVM pass manager. Use `glyim_test::snapshot` to compare assembly output.

name: End‑to‑End Monomorphization Pipeline Integration
scope_summary: Integrate mono item collection, caching, codegen unit partitioning into the main pipeline. Replace placeholder with real monomorphization. Enable parallel code generation per CGU using rayon. Ensure error propagation and diagnostics.

tests.V33-T01: Generic function used from multiple crates → mono items collected (run-pass)
tests.V33-T02: Parallel codegen over CGUs → correct output (run-pass)
tests.V33-T03: Cached mono items reused across compilations (run-pass)
tests.V33-T04: End‑to‑end compilation of generic library + binary (run-pass)

mocking: Real Database and backend. Use `glyim_test::TestRunner` with `--test` flag.
